message.content:
READ-ONLY deep refactoring audit of the Locations page of the Evolution One CMS (Next.js App Router, MongoDB/Mongoose, TypeScript). DO NOT edit any files. Only read and report.

Entry point: `app/locations/page.tsx` and its detail route `app/locations/[slug]/page.tsx`. Trace the full tree: PageContent component(s) under `components/CMS/locations/` (search for actual location), all child components, all custom hooks (lib/hooks/locations/, lib/hooks/data/useLocation*), all API routes called (app/api/locations, app/api/locationAggregation, etc.), and all helpers/utils/types imported (lib/helpers, lib/utils, app/api/lib/helpers, shared/types, lib/types, app/api/lib/types).

Audit against these project rules (CLAUDE.md + .instructions + .claude/skills):
- TYPE SAFETY: zero `any`, `type` not `interface`, `.lean<T>()` mandatory on Mongoose queries, no `Record<string,unknown>` for domain data, explicit return types, NO comments in type files.
- NAMING: `[PageName]PageContent`, `[PageName][Section][Component]`, `Props` suffix, handle/fetch/format/calculate/map prefixes, no single-letter vars, use-case file names.
- MONGOOSE: `.lean<T>()`, `.aggregate<T>()`, `Meters.aggregate().cursor({batchSize:1000})` NOT `.exec()`, types from shared/types, use `location` field directly (no $lookup on Meters), no findById, findOne({_id}).
- API ROUTES: file-level JSDoc @module, numbered STEP comments with ===== separators, withApiAuth wrapper, helper extraction >30 lines, route <500 / helper <600 lines, performance tracking Date.now() warn >1000ms, getUserLocationFilter() licencee filtering (support both `licencee` spellings), getMoneyInScale/getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale reviewer scaling applied to ALL financial returns (never inline). Note: locationAggregation route is known to use the split money-in/out scale helpers correctly — verify other location routes do too.
- GAMING DAY: 8AM offset via lib/utils/gamingDayRange.ts, global range + in-memory filter, store gameDayOffset not hardcoded 8.
- CURRENCY: never hardcode `$`, formatCurrencyWithCodeString(amount, displayCurrency), pass ?currency= to APIs, chart tooltip formatters too.
- REACT/NEXT: direct React imports (no React namespace), section comments `// =====`, skeleton loaders matching exact layout (no spinners), financial color via getFinancialColorClass/getGrossColorClass, useMemo/useCallback.
- HOOK STRUCTURE: 13-section order.
- FILE ORG: shared/types vs lib/types vs app/api/lib/types; lib/helpers vs app/api/lib/helpers; redundancy/duplication; files over limits.
- SECURITY: location filter on all scoped queries, reviewer scale, session invalidation, conditional cookie secure (no hardcoded secure:true).
- PERFORMANCE: cursor, N+1, batch queries.
- REDUNDANCY: unused imports/exports/types (grep to confirm), duplicate types, dead code, files to merge/split.

Return:
## Locations Findings
Markdown table: Category | File (path) | Issue | Severity (Critical/High/Med/Low) | Concrete Fix.
## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)
## Files read (full list)
Be specific with paths and line numbers. Prioritize real, verifiable issues; note convention violations too. Flag anything you could NOT fully verify. Do NOT make changes.

attachment.content:
- api-route-structure: Structure API routes with step-by-step comments, JSDoc, helper extraction, and proper organization.
- backend-standards: Mongoose models over direct collections, findOne() not findById(), licencee filtering, aggregation patterns.
- code-style-eslint: Never ignore ESLint violations, run lint regularly, file-level documentation, remove redundant comments.
- database-query-optimization: Use .cursor() for Meters, direct location field access, eliminate N+1 patterns, handle gaming day offsets.
- file-organization: Directory structure for shared code, frontend, backend, components, and utilities across the application.
- gaming-day-offset-system: 8 AM to 8 AM business day (Trinidad UTC-4), gaming day calculations, custom date handling, per-location offsets.
- licencee-access-control: Multi-tenant access control, role-based permissions, location filtering, session invalidation on permission changes.
- local-app-testing: Logging in and manually testing the Evolution One CMS in a real browser on localhost. Use when driving the app via the browser (Claude in Chrome) to verify a change, test a flow, or reproduce a bug. Contains the dev login credentials.
- page-component-structure: page.tsx thin wrappers, component organization with section comments, and JSX formatting patterns.
- react-typescript-rules: React imports, TypeScript discipline, state management patterns, and type organization rules.
- skeleton-loaders: Content-specific skeleton loaders that exactly match real content layout. NEVER use generic spinners or "Loading..." text.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll audit the Locations page. Let me start by tracing the entry points.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Locations Page
3	 * Main entry point for the gaming locations management section.
4	 *
5	 * This page displays a comprehensive list of gaming locations with financial
6	 * metrics, machine status, and management tools. It delegates core logic
7	 * to LocationsPageContent.
8	 *
9	 * @module app/locations/page
10	 */
11	
12	import LocationsPageContent from '@/components/CMS/locations/LocationsPageContent';
13	import ProtectedRoute from '@/components/shared/auth/ProtectedRoute';
14	import MaintenanceWrapper from '@/components/shared/maintenance/MaintenanceWrapper';
15	import PageErrorBoundary from '@/components/shared/ui/errors/PageErrorBoundary';
16	
17	export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
18	
19	export default function LocationsPage() {
20	  return (
21	    <ProtectedRoute requiredPage="locations">
22	      <MaintenanceWrapper pageKey="locations">
23	        <PageErrorBoundary>
24	          <LocationsPageContent />
25	        </PageErrorBoundary>
26	      </MaintenanceWrapper>
27	    </ProtectedRoute>
28	  );
29	}
30

content.text: This is a substantial tree. Let me read the core components and hooks in parallel batches.
Now let me look at the API routes and key helpers/types. Let me read the main location routes and the aggregation route.

Notable: `locationAggregation/route.ts` does NOT use `withApiAuth` (uses manual `getUserFromServer`), calls `getUserFromServer()` twice, and has out-of-order STEP numbers (4.5, 5, 6 after 7...). Let me check the key helpers and the locationAggregation helper for the Meters query patterns, plus the `[locationId]` operations helper and remaining components.

I have strong coverage of the core data flow. Key issue found: `locationAggregation.ts` uses `.exec()` on `GamingLocations.aggregate(basePipeline)` (line 299) and `.aggregate(...).exec()` for the basicList path (1145, 1273) — those are GamingLocations not Meters so allowed, but the Meters cursor usage is correct. Let me check the remaining components, the membership-count route, search routes, and the types files. Let me also check `lib/types/location.ts` and key utils.

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Locations Search API Route
3	 *
4	 * This route handles searching locations with financial metrics.
5	 * It supports:
6	 * - Location name search
7	 * - Licencee filtering
8	 * - Time period filtering
9	 * - Financial metrics aggregation (money in, money out, gross)
10	 * - Machine statistics (total, online)
11	 *
12	 * @module app/api/locations/search/route
13	 */
14	
15	import { connectDB } from '@/app/api/lib/middleware/db';
16	import { Meters } from '@/app/api/lib/models/meters';
17	import { LocationResponse, MeterMatchStage } from '@/lib/types/location';
18	import {
19	  logRouteFetch,
20	  logRouteError,
21	  extractUserFromRequest,
22	} from '@/app/api/lib/utils/routeLogger';
23	import type { PipelineStage } from 'mongoose';
24	import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
25	/**
26	 * Main GET handler for searching locations
27	 *
28	 * @param {string} licencee - Filter search by licencee name
29	 * @param {string} search - Search query for location name
30	 * @param {string} startDate - ISO date for metrics range start (default: 30d ago)
31	 * @param {string} endDate - ISO date for metrics range end (default: now)
32	 *
33	 * Flow:
34	 * 1. Parse query parameters (licencee, timePeriod, search, dates)
35	 * 2. Build location match filter
36	 * 3. Connect to database
37	 * 4. Build metrics aggregation pipeline
38	 * 5. Fetch location data with machine statistics
39	 * 6. Combine location and metrics data
40	 * 7. Return search results
41	 */
42	export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
43	  const startTime = Date.now();
44	  const functionName = 'GET /api/locations/search';
45	  const user = extractUserFromRequest(request);
46	
47	  try {
48	    // ============================================================================
49	    // STEP 1: Parse query parameters
50	    // ============================================================================
51	    const searchParams = new URL(request.url).searchParams;
52	    const licencee = searchParams.get('licencee') ?? '';
53	    const search = searchParams.get('search')?.trim() || '';
54	
55	    // ============================================================================
56	    // STEP 2: Build location match filter
57	    // ============================================================================
58	    const locationMatch: Record<string, unknown> = {
59	      $or: [
60	        { deletedAt: null },
61	        { deletedAt: { $lt: new Date('2025-01-01') } },
62	      ],
63	    };
64	
65	    if (search) {
66	      locationMatch.name = { $regex: search, $options: 'i' };
67	    }
68	    if (licencee) {
69	      if (!locationMatch.$and) {
70	        locationMatch.$and = [];
71	      }
72	      (locationMatch.$and as unknown[]).push({
73	        $or: [{ 'rel.licencee': licencee }, { 'rel.licencee': licencee }],
74	      });
75	    }
76	
77	    // ============================================================================
78	    // STEP 3: Connect to database
79	    // ============================================================================
80	    const db = await connectDB();
81	    if (!db) {
82	      return NextResponse.json(
83	        { success: false, message: 'DB connection failed' },
84	        { status: 500 }
85	      );
86	    }
87	
88	    // ============================================================================
89	    // STEP 4: Build metrics aggregation pipeline
90	    // ============================================================================
91	    const { searchParams: searchParamsFromRequest } = new URL(request.url);
92	    const startDate = new Date(
93	      searchParamsFromRequest.get('startDate') ??
94	        Date.now() - 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000
95	    );
96	    const endDate = new Date(
97	      searchParamsFromRequest.get('endDate') ?? Date.now()
98	    );
99	
100	    const matchStage: MeterMatchStage = {
101	      readAt: { $gte: startDate, $lte: endDate },
102	    };
103	    if (licencee) {
104	      (matchStage as Record<string, unknown>).$or = [
105	        { 'rel.licencee': licencee },
106	        { 'rel.licencee': licencee },
107	      ];
108	    }
109	
110	    // ============================================================================
111	    // STEP 5 & 6: Single aggregation combining meters, machines, and locations
112	    // ============================================================================
113	    // 🚀 OPTIMIZED: Single aggregation starting from Meters, joining to machines, then locations
114	    // This eliminates the need for 2 separate queries and in-memory combination
115	    const threeMinutesAgo = new Date(Date.now() - 3 * 60 * 1000);
116	
117	    const aggregationPipeline: PipelineStage[] = [
118	      // Stage 1: Start from Meters - filter by date range
119	      { $match: matchStage },
120	      // Stage 2: Join to machines to get gamingLocation
121	      {
122	        $lookup: {
123	          from: 'machines',
124	          localField: 'machine',
125	          foreignField: '_id',
126	          as: 'machineDetails',
127	        },
128	      },
129	      {
130	        $unwind: {
131	          path: '$machineDetails',
132	          preserveNullAndEmptyArrays: false,
133	        },
134	      },
135	      // Stage 3: Join to locations to apply location filters and get location data
136	      {
137	        $lookup: {
138	          from: 'gaminglocations',
139	          localField: 'machineDetails.gamingLocation',
140	          foreignField: '_id',
141	          as: 'locationDetails',
142	        },
143	      },
144	      {
145	        $unwind: {
146	          path: '$locationDetails',
147	          preserveNullAndEmptyArrays: false,
148	        },
149	      },
150	      // Stage 4: Apply location filters (deletion status, search, licencee)
151	      // Note: locationMatch already includes deletion status, search, and licencee filters
152	      { $match: locationMatch },
153	      // Stage 5: Group by location to calculate financial metrics and machine stats
154	      {
155	        $group: {
156	          _id: '$locationDetails._id',
157	          // Location fields
158	          name: { $first: '$locationDetails.name' },
159	          address: { $first: '$locationDetails.address' },
160	          country: { $first: '$locationDetails.country' },
161	          rel: { $first: '$locationDetails.rel' },
162	          profitShare: { $first: '$locationDetails.profitShare' },
163	          geoCoords: { $first: '$locationDetails.geoCoords' },
164	          isLocalServer: { $first: '$locationDetails.isLocalServer' },
165	          // Financial metrics from meters
166	          moneyIn: { $sum: { $ifNull: ['$movement.drop', 0] } },
167	          moneyOut: {
168	            $sum: { $ifNull: ['$movement.totalCancelledCredits', 0] },
169	          },
170	          // Machine statistics (count unique machines and online status)
171	          machineIds: { $addToSet: '$machineDetails._id' },
172	          aceEnabled: { $first: '$locationDetails.aceEnabled' },
173	          onlineMachineIds: {
174	            $addToSet: {
175	              $cond: [
176	                {
177	                  $or: [
178	                    { $eq: ['$locationDetails.aceEnabled', true] },
179	                    {
180	                      $and: [
181	                        { $ne: ['$machineDetails.lastActivity', null] },
182	                        {
183	                          $gte: [
184	                            '$machineDetails.lastActivity',
185	                            threeMinutesAgo,
186	                          ],
187	                        },
188	                      ],
189	                    },
190	                  ],
191	                },
192	                '$machineDetails._id',
193	                '$$REMOVE',
194	              ],
195	            },
196	          },
197	        },
198	      },
199	      // Stage 6: Calculate derived fields
200	      {
201	        $addFields: {
202	          gross: { $subtract: ['$moneyIn', '$moneyOut'] },
203	          totalMachines: { $size: '$machineIds' },
204	          onlineMachines: { $size: '$onlineMachineIds' },
205	        },
206	      },
207	      // Stage 7: Project final fields
208	      {
209	        $project: {
210	          _id: 1,
211	          name: 1,
212	          address: 1,
213	          country: 1,
214	          rel: 1,
215	          profitShare: 1,
216	          geoCoords: 1,
217	          totalMachines: 1,
218	          onlineMachines: 1,
219	          moneyIn: 1,
220	          moneyOut: 1,
221	          gross: 1,
222	          isLocalServer: { $ifNull: ['$isLocalServer', false] },
223	          hasSmib: { $gt: ['$totalMachines', 0] }, // Simplified - assumes machines with meters have SMIB
224	        },
225	      },
226	    ];
227	
228	    // Use cursor for performance (MANDATORY for Meters aggregations)
229	    const locations: Array<
230	      LocationResponse & { moneyIn?: number; moneyOut?: number }
231	    > = [];
232	    const cursor = Meters.aggregate<
233	      LocationResponse & { moneyIn?: number; moneyOut?: number }
234	    >(aggregationPipeline).cursor({ batchSize: 1000 });
235	
236	    for await (const doc of cursor) {
237	      locations.push(
238	        doc as LocationResponse & { moneyIn?: number; moneyOut?: number }
239	      );
240	    }
241	
242	    // ============================================================================
243	    // STEP 7: Return search results
244	    // ============================================================================
245	    const response = locations.map(loc => ({
246	      _id: loc._id,
247	      locationName: loc.name,
248	      country: loc.country,
249	      address: loc.address,
250	      rel: loc.rel,
251	      profitShare: loc.profitShare,
252	      geoCoords: loc.geoCoords,
253	      totalMachines: loc.totalMachines || 0,
254	      onlineMachines: loc.onlineMachines || 0,
255	      moneyIn: (loc as { moneyIn?: number }).moneyIn || 0,
256	      moneyOut: (loc as { moneyOut?: number }).moneyOut || 0,
257	      gross: loc.gross || 0,
258	      isLocalServer: loc.isLocalServer || false,
259	      hasSmib: loc.hasSmib || false,
260	    }));
261	
262	    const duration = Date.now() - startTime;
263	    logRouteFetch(
264	      functionName,
265	      'GET',
266	      '/api/locations/search',
267	      response.length,
268	      user,
269	      duration
270	    );
271	
272	    if (duration > 2000) {
273	      console.warn(`[Locations Search API] Completed in ${duration}ms`);
274	    }
275	
276	    return NextResponse.json(response);
277	  } catch (error) {
278	    const duration = Date.now() - startTime;
279	    const errorMessage =
280	      error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Internal server error';
281	    logRouteError(
282	      functionName,
283	      'GET',
284	      '/api/locations/search',
285	      errorMessage,
286	      user
287	    );
288	    console.error(
289	      `[Locations Search API] Error after ${duration}ms:`,
290	      errorMessage
291	    );
292	    return NextResponse.json(
293	      { success: false, message: errorMessage },
294	      { status: 500 }
295	    );

content.text:
Confirmed: `/api/locations/search` has zero callers in the codebase (only self-references) — it's dead code. And `LocationsLocationTable` is not passed `selectedFilters` from PageContent, so the "no recent collection report" warning icon (gated on `selectedFilters.some(f => f === 'NoSMIBLocation')`) never renders in the table. I have enough to compile the report.

## Locations Findings

| Category | File (path) | Issue | Severity | Concrete Fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Security / Auth | `app/api/locations/search/route.ts` | Route has **no `withApiAuth`**, **no auth check at all**, and **no `getUserLocationFilter`** licencee/location scoping. It aggregates Meters → machines → all gaminglocations and returns financial metrics for ANY location matching name/licencee. Cross-tenant data leakage. | **Critical** | Either delete the route (see Redundancy below) or wrap in `withApiAuth` and apply `getUserLocationFilter` to the `locationMatch`. |
| Redundancy / Dead code | `app/api/locations/search/route.ts` | Route has **zero callers** — the page tree uses `/api/locations/search-all` (via `lib/helpers/locations.ts:126`). Only self-references in grep. | **High** | Delete the entire route file. This also resolves the Critical auth finding. |
| Auth consistency | `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts` | Does **not** use `withApiAuth`; rolls its own `getUserFromServer()` + 401 (lines 244-247). Also calls `getUserFromServer()` **twice** (line 244 and again line 373) — duplicate DB/JWT work per request. Plus a dev-mode `testUserId` auth bypass (line 213-241) that ships to prod code. | High | Migrate to `withApiAuth` to get `user`/`userRoles`/`isAdminOrDev` once; remove the second `getUserFromServer()` call; gate or remove the `testUserId` bypass. |
| API structure | `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts` | STEP comments are out of order/duplicated: STEP 4.5 (line 266) appears after STEP 5 (210), STEP 6 (307) after STEP 7 (341); two "STEP 10" blocks (363, 1053-ref). JSDoc flow lists 10 steps but body diverges. | Med | Renumber steps sequentially to match execution and the JSDoc flow. |
| API structure / Auth | `app/api/locations/search-all/route.ts` | No `withApiAuth` wrapper (uses manual `getUserFromServer`), though it DOES correctly apply `getUserLocationFilter` and split reviewer scales. No `Date.now() > 1000ms` warn (only `>2000`). | Med | Wrap in `withApiAuth` for consistency; align perf warn threshold to 1000ms per CLAUDE.md. |
| API structure | `app/api/locations/membership-count/route.ts` | No `withApiAuth` (manual `getUserFromServer`); STEP label typo `STEP1` (line 43); no perf warn. Otherwise applies location filter correctly. | Med | Wrap in `withApiAuth`; fix step label; add `>1000ms` warn. |
| Mongoose typing | `app/api/lib/helpers/locationAggregation.ts` | `GamingLocations.aggregate(basePipeline).exec()` (line 299) and the basicList `.aggregate([...]).exec()` (line 1145/1273) use **no generic type** — results are implicitly `any[]`, driving the many `as unknown as AggregatedLocation` casts. (Meters cursors ARE correct.) | Med | Add `.aggregate<LocationAggRow>(...)` generics sourced from a shared type so downstream casts can be removed. |
| Type safety | `app/api/lib/helpers/locationAggregation.ts` | Pervasive `as unknown as AggregatedLocation` (lines 612, 641, 968, 1327) and `as unknown as { sasMachines: number }` / `{ locationName: string }` (1081, 1104). Domain objects built as untyped literals then double-cast. | Med | Define a concrete builder return type matching `AggregatedLocation` and return it directly. |
| Type safety | `app/api/locations/route.ts` | Repeated `userPayload as Record<string, unknown>`, `currentUser as { _id; emailAddress }`, `(error as Record<string, unknown>).statusCode`. Domain data passed as `Record<string,unknown>` to handlers. | Low | Type the handler params (`LeanUserDocument`) and use a typed error shape (`{ statusCode?: number }`). |
| Type safety | `app/api/lib/helpers/locations/locationByIdOperations.ts` | Heavy field-level casts on `machine.*` (`as string`, `as Date | null`, `(machine.custom as unknown as {name?})`); `paginateAndTransformCabinets` returns `as unknown as TransformedCabinet`. Indicates `GamingMachine` type is loose or mismatched. | Low | Tighten `GamingMachine`/`TransformedCabinet` so casts are unnecessary; map explicitly. |
| Type safety | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationCabinetsData.ts` | `locationData` accessed via untyped axios response (`locationResponse.data?.location`), then `as AggregatedLocation`; large inline error-shape casts (`as Error & {status?; response?}`) repeated 3x. | Low | Type the axios responses; extract a shared `isUnauthorizedError(err)` helper to remove duplicate cast blocks. |
| Naming | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationsPageData.ts` | `_setSmibSyncStatus` (line 65) uses an underscore prefix — violates "no underscore-prefixed variables except `_id`". | Low | Rename to `setSmibSyncStatus` (it IS used at line 273). |
| Currency | `components/CMS/locations/LocationsLocationTable.tsx` | Jackpot cell hardcodes `text-amber-600` for the value but that's fine; however currency is correctly routed through `formatCurrency` prop. No `$` hardcode found. ✓ (no issue) | — | — |
| React / dead prop | `components/CMS/locations/LocationsLocationTable.tsx` + `LocationsPageContent.tsx` | Table renders the "No recent collection report" warning icon only when `selectedFilters.some(f => f === 'NoSMIBLocation')` (line 286), but `LocationsPageContent` **never passes `selectedFilters`** to `LocationsLocationTable` (only to the filter section). The warning icon is therefore dead in the table view. | Med | Pass `selectedFilters={selectedFilters}` to `LocationsLocationTable`, or drop the filter gate if the icon should always show. |
| Type safety / dead | `lib/types/location/types.ts` | `LocationTableProps.isDeveloper` and `LocationCardData.isDeveloper` are defined but the component uses `canPermanentlyDelete` instead — `isDeveloper` is an unused/legacy prop. | Low | Remove `isDeveloper` from both types. |
| Type files: no comments | `lib/types/location/types.ts` | Inline comments on type members (lines 101-102: `// If false, hide edit/delete buttons`, `// If true, show permanent delete...`) violate "No comments in type files". | Low | Remove the comments; names are self-documenting. |
| React state | `components/CMS/locations/LocationsPageContent.tsx` | `key={String(loc._id \|\| loc.location \|\| Math.random())}` (line 269) — `Math.random()` as a React key breaks reconciliation/causes remounts when id is missing. Same pattern in `LocationsLocationTable.tsx` (line 154). | Med | Fall back to array index or a stable composite key, never `Math.random()`. |
| Type safety | `components/CMS/locations/LocationsPageContent.tsx` | `handleRestore` casts `location as Record<string, unknown>` then reads `.location`/`.locationName` as strings (lines 115-117) despite `AggregatedLocation` already typing these. | Low | Use `AggregatedLocation` fields directly without the cast. |
| Hook structure / size | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationCabinetsData.ts` | 1017 lines, far beyond comfortable hook size; the giant batch-loading effect (304-477) duplicates the same `onlineStatus` conversion + `customDateRange` from/to mapping **three times** (current batch, next batch, main fetch). | Med | Extract `mapCustomRange(customDateRange)` and `toOnlineStatus(selectedStatus)` helpers; consider splitting batch-fetch logic into a sub-hook. |
| Redundancy | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationCabinetsData.ts` | Duplicate comment line 288-289 (`// Update allCabinets when accumulatedCabinets changes` twice). `effectivePage = debouncedSearchTerm?.trim() ? 1 : 1` (line 738) is a no-op ternary. | Low | Remove duplicate comment; simplify `effectivePage = 1`. |
| Performance | `app/api/lib/helpers/locationAggregation.ts` | The non-`useSingleAggregation` path (Today/Yesterday/Custom) processes locations in batches of 10 with a Meters cursor per batch — correct pattern, but it re-runs `getGamingDayRangeForPeriod` and re-fetches machines per batch rather than once globally (the 7d/30d path already does the global approach). | Low | Consider unifying both paths on the global-range + in-memory-filter approach used for 7d/30d. |
| Gaming day | `app/api/lib/helpers/locationAggregation.ts`, `app/api/locations/[locationId]/route.ts` | Gaming day offset correctly read from `location.gameDayOffset ?? 8` (uses `??`, respects 0). ✓ No hardcoded 8 in query logic. | — | (compliant) |
| Reviewer scale | `app/api/locations/[locationId]/route.ts`, `search-all`, `locationAggregation` | All three correctly use split `getMoneyInScale`/`getMoneyOutAndJackpotScale` (via `computeReviewerScales`) applied after currency conversion. ✓ | — | (compliant — verified per task note) |
| Chart currency | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationChartData.ts` | Passes `?currency=displayCurrency` to `/api/analytics/location-trends` and notes reviewer scaling is server-side. ✓ Transform maps drop/totalCancelledCredits/gross correctly. No client `$`. | — | (compliant) |
| Type safety | `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationChartData.ts` | The trends `.map` callback types the item with an inline index signature `[locationId: string]: {...} | string | undefined` (lines 421-436) instead of a shared `LocationTrendPoint` type. | Low | Move to a shared `LocationTrendPoint` type in `shared/types`. |

## Proposed Actions (ordered, concrete)

1. **Delete `app/api/locations/search/route.ts`** — it's dead (zero callers) AND unauthenticated/unscoped. Removing it closes the Critical data-leak and the High dead-code finding in one move. Verify e2e (`locations.spec.ts`) doesn't hit it first (grep shows it does not).
2. **Migrate `locationAggregation`, `search-all`, and `membership-count` routes to `withApiAuth`** — removes the double `getUserFromServer()` in `locationAggregation`, normalizes 401 handling, and gates/removes the `testUserId` dev bypass. Keep their existing (correct) `getUserLocationFilter` + reviewer-scale logic.
3. **Fix the table warning-icon wiring**: pass `selectedFilters` from `LocationsPageContent` to `LocationsLocationTable` (or remove the gate) so `hasNoRecentCollectionReport` renders.
4. **Replace `Math.random()` React keys** in `LocationsPageContent.tsx` (269) and `LocationsLocationTable.tsx` (154) with stable fallbacks.
5. **Add aggregate generics** to `GamingLocations.aggregate(...)` calls in `locationAggregation.ts` and define a concrete builder return type to eliminate the `as unknown as AggregatedLocation` casts.
6. **Refactor `useLocationCabinetsData.ts`**: extract `toOnlineStatus()` and `mapCustomRange()` helpers (3x duplication), fix the `effectivePage` no-op, remove the duplicate comment; consider splitting batch loading into a sub-hook to get under size limits.
7. **Type-file cleanup** (`lib/types/location/types.ts`): remove inline member comments, drop unused `isDeveloper`.
8. **Renumber STEP comments** in `locationAggregation/route.ts` to match execution order; fix `STEP1` typo in `membership-count`; align perf-warn thresholds to 1000ms.
9. **Rename `_setSmibSyncStatus` → `setSmibSyncStatus`** in `useLocationsPageData.ts`.

## Could NOT fully verify / flags
- **`shared/types` AggregatedLocation / GamingMachine / TransformedCabinet shapes**: I inferred looseness from the volume of casts but did not open these type definitions. The cast findings (rows on `locationByIdOperations.ts`, `locationAggregation.ts`) should be confirmed against the actual type fields before refactoring.
- **`getUserLocationFilter` "both licencee spellings"**: the routes pass a single `licencee` param; I did not open `licenceeFilter.ts` to confirm it internally handles the alternate spelling. `search-all`/`membership-count`/`locationAggregation` all delegate to it, so coverage depends on that helper.
- I did not exhaustively read every leaf component (`LocationsCabinetGrid`, `LocationsLocationCard`, mobile card, modals, map picker, members/cabinets sections, skeletons). The audit focused on the data-flow spine (pages → hooks → routes → helpers → types). Those components warrant a follow-up pass, particularly for currency-format and skeleton-match compliance.
- The dev-mode `testUserId` bypass in `locationAggregation` is gated on `NODE_ENV === 'development'`, so it is not exploitable in prod, but it's still a code smell worth removing.

### Files read (full list)
- `app/locations/page.tsx`
- `app/locations/[slug]/page.tsx`
- `components/CMS/locations/LocationsPageContent.tsx`
- `components/CMS/locations/LocationsDetailsPageContent.tsx`
- `components/CMS/locations/LocationsLocationTable.tsx`
- `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationsPageData.ts`
- `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationChartData.ts`
- `lib/hooks/locations/useLocationCabinetsData.ts`
- `app/api/locations/route.ts`

- `app/api/locations/search/route.ts`
- `app/api/locations/search-all/route.ts`
- `app/api/locations/membership-count/route.ts`
- `app/api/locationAggregation/route.ts`
- `app/api/lib/helpers/locationAggregation.ts`
- `app/api/lib/helpers/locations/locationByIdOperations.ts`
- `lib/types/location/types.ts`
- `shared/types/auth.ts` (partial)
